title: Fluffy Golden Tunisian Bread Gratin
description: Sweet and savory Tunisian bread gratin that is perfect as a post-workout snack or dessert. Bitterness from the gherkin balances out the sweetness of the caramel sauce.
instructions: Cut the Tunisian bread into 1-inch cubes and set aside.
In a separate bowl, mix together the nuka-pickle, caramel sauce, egg, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
In a greased 8x8-inch baking dish, arrange half of the bread cubes.
Pour the nuka-pickle mixture over the bread, followed by half of the mozzarella cheese.
Repeat the layering process, starting with the remaining bread cubes, then the remaining nuka-pickle mixture, and finally the remaining mozzarella cheese.
Sprinkle chopped parsley and bitter gherkin over the top.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
